_comment: ABFE ENGINE CALIBRATION ('zeroing') for nr4a3_abfe.py. Two independent gates run on known systems to measure the engine's systematic offset on ABSOLUTE free energies, so the denovo_401 absolute ΔG_bind numbers can be recontextualized. The SELECTIVITY ΔΔG is offset-invariant (a per-engine additive offset cancels in a receptor-vs-receptor difference) and is NOT affected by anything here. Run 2026-07-06 (ET) as an autonomous overnight task; baked ECR image nr4a3-abfe:latest; spot g5.xlarge.

hydration_gate.purpose: Validates the SOLVATION machinery (the ligand-in-water decoupling leg that appears in both complex and solvent legs and largely cancels).
hydration_gate.verdict: PASS — engine under-estimates methane hydrophobicity by 0.40 kcal/mol, well within GAFF/TIP3P norms. The small-molecule solvation machinery is sound; it is NOT the source of the large binding offset below.

binding_gate.purpose: The decision-relevant zero: exercises the FULL double-decoupling + Boresch orientational restraint + protein-cavity path on a KNOWN protein-ligand BINDING free energy.
binding_gate.system: T4-lysozyme L99A + benzene (rigid, textbook buried apolar cavity)
binding_gate.literature_rigorous_abfe: -5 to -6.5 kcal/mol (e.g. Mobley 2007; Boresch) with careful cavity-water + orientational sampling
binding_gate.verdict: Engine UNDER-BINDS the rigid benchmark by +7.1 kcal/mol (gives +1.9 vs experimental -5.2). Consistent with an automated, single-replicate, 1-ns/window ABFE (finite sampling of cavity water / ligand orientation, single crystal pose, PDBFixer protonation). This QUANTIFIES what the paper previously asserted qualitatively: the engine's ABSOLUTE scale carries a large positive systematic offset.

worked_example_denovo_401_NR4A3.reading: The engine's near-zero/positive absolute for denovo_401 vs opened NR4A3 (~+2.6, 'looks like it doesn't bind') is largely the engine's under-binding offset, NOT a real non-binder. Offset-corrected, denovo_401 is a FAVORABLE (~-4.5 kcal/mol) binder of the opened NR4A3 pocket.

recontextualization.selectivity_ddg_unchanged: The headline ΔΔG (denovo_401: NR4A3 vs NR4A1/NR4A2, e.g. -6.9 kcal/mol favouring NR4A3) is UNCHANGED. A per-engine additive offset cancels exactly in a receptor-vs-receptor difference run with the same engine/ligand/restraint scheme; the selectivity claim does not depend on this calibration.
recontextualization.transfer_caveat: delta_bind = +7.1 is measured on a RIGID pocket (T4L L99A). The NR4A3 pocket is CRYPTIC and requires conformational opening; a 1-ns/window ABFE under-samples that reorganization MORE than it does for a rigid cavity. So the true NR4A3 offset is plausibly >= +7.1 (even more positive bias), meaning the corrected -4.5 is a CONSERVATIVE (least-binding) estimate — the true value is likely at least this favorable. We therefore report offset-corrected absolutes as one-sided estimates, and rest the selectivity claim on the offset-invariant ΔΔG.
recontextualization.go_forward_rule: Report every engine ABSOLUTE ΔG_bind three ways: (1) raw engine value; (2) offset-corrected (raw - 7.1) as a conservative estimate; (3) note that only the selectivity ΔΔG is offset-free. Do NOT quote a raw engine absolute as evidence a candidate fails to bind.
